### Post by izkkk on 2012-03-22
Hello,

i have ubuntu 11.10 computer with 2 nics, so it gets internet by eth0, and share it by eth1 with other computer (windows xp)

i have problem, because 2nd computer is not getting internet before i manually go to terminal and execute 2 commands:
sudo ifdown eth1
sudo ifup eth1

And then it works. Question is - how can i make it automatically?

And here my logs:
```

sudo iptables-save
# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.10 on Sat Mar 10 15:41:50 2012

:PREROUTING ACCEPT [5674:762521]
```

### Post by Insomn1a on 2012-03-22
>      RX packets:103 errors:5 dropped:10 overruns:5 frame:0 


Your getting errors on that NIC do you have any wireless routers that might be using the same IP address as Eth1, or any other devices?

your basically disabling and re-enabling your NIC's from what i understand each time. Try changing the IP address of ETH1 to something else like 192.168.1.1 and see if that will work. also, does this computer broadcast DHCP and DNS?

---

### Post by izkkk on 2012-03-25
Thanks for help. I changed ip adresse to 192.168.1.1, but problem is still here.

```
$ cat /etc/network/interfaces
auto lo
iface lo inet loopback
post-up iptables-restore /etc/iptables.conf
auto eth0
iface eth0 inet dhcp
auto eth1
iface eth1 inet static
address 192.168.1.1
network 192.168.1.0
netmask 255.255.255.0
broadcast 192.168.1.255

```

The problem is that, i need to type commands ifdown eth1, ifup eth1, every day to share my internet on the second computer, but i think it should autostart.

And on the second computer i wrote ip address 192.168.1.101, netmask 255.255.255.0, gateway 192.168.1.1, and the same dns of my internet provider as on my first computer.

So i did not broadcast dhcp and dns.

---

### Post by collisionystm on 2012-03-25
Your iptables rule is wrong

-A POSTROUTING -s 192.168.0.0/24 ! -d 192.168.0.0/24 -j MASQUERADE 


You need to have

-A POSTROUTING -s 192.168.0.0/24 ! -i EXTERNAL.INTERFACE -j MASQUERADE

---

>

```
$ cat /etc/iptables.conf
```

---

### Post by collisionystm on 2012-03-26
Remove the ! mark you have in there.

-A POSTROUTING -s 192.168.1.0/24 -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE

---

### Post by izkkk on 2012-03-31
This does not help.

Problem is solved by change NICs cables and NICs mac addresses: eth1 => eth0, eth0 => eth1

---
